title: Schema: tennis preferences, preferred partners, captain private notes, user-player link
description: Land the data model that underpins both Sprint 016 and Sprint 017. Migration 024 creates player_tennis_preferences (one-to-one with player, all nullable columns), player_preferred_partners (join table, positive-only), and captain_player_notes (captain-only, never queried from player handlers). Migration 025 adds users.player_id so an admin/captain can be linked to their player record for Sprint 017 personalisation.
acceptance_criteria: Migration 024 creates player_tennis_preferences with one nullable row per player, covering every field in the taxonomy below
Migration 024 creates player_preferred_partners (player_id, partner_player_id, kind ENUM 'clicks_with' | 'would_love_to_try', created_at) — positive kinds ONLY
Migration 024 creates captain_player_notes (id, player_id, author_user_id, kind ENUM 'partnership' | 'general', body TEXT, created_at, updated_at)
No 'avoid_partner' column anywhere — negative/tactical info lives exclusively on captain_player_notes
Migration 025 adds users.player_id (UUID, nullable, FK to players.id)
All migrations have matching .down.sql files that cleanly reverse
Running migrate-down from clean state removes all new tables/columns without touching pre-existing data
Model structs added: PlayerTennisPreferences, PlayerPreferredPartner, CaptainPlayerNote, and User.PlayerID

files_to_modify: internal/models/models.go — add the three new structs; add PlayerID to User

match_type_preferences: same_gender_doubles_appetite TEXT enum (love/happy/prefer_not/no_thanks) — UI shows mens or womens based on Player.Gender
intensity_and_goals: competitiveness INTEGER 1-5 (1 pure fun, 5 want to win)
improvement_focus TEXT — JSON array of strings (net_play/serve/movement/mental/fitness/consistency/doubles_iq)
logistics: preferred_days TEXT — JSON array of strings (mon/tue/wed/thu/fri)
preferred_times TEXT — JSON array of strings (morning/afternoon/evening/anytime)
health_and_access: what_to_know_about_my_game TEXT — positive framing of injuries/knocks; captain-visible
communications: notes_to_captain TEXT — positive/logistical notes from player, captain-readable

key_decisions: One-to-one table keyed by player_id (UUID) rather than JSON blob — Sprint 017 needs to filter/sort by handedness, court side, etc.
JSON TEXT columns only for intrinsically list-shaped values (preferred_days, preferred_times, improvement_focus)
captain_player_notes is isolated — no package under internal/players/* should import its repository. Enforced by convention and a leak-regression E2E test in Sprint 017 WI-107
user.player_id is nullable — existing admin accounts keep working unlinked

testing_requirements: Migration applies cleanly on a fresh database and on a populated copy
Down migration removes all new tables/columns without touching pre-existing data
Model structs marshal/unmarshal JSON correctly for list-shaped columns
Repository unit test (added in WI-095/WI-098 scope) confirms insert + update of all enum fields

context.user_story: As the system, I need a rich but optional data model for how a player describes their tennis, plus a quiet captain-only note space for anything players shouldn't have to express about themselves.
design_principles: Celebrate difference — the taxonomy leans into what makes each player themselves
Positive-only for anything the player can see; no 'avoid' fields on player-authored tables
Every field optional — partial completion is the expected state
